mnemonics: NASH: No Agent Switches Strategy Happily (in a Nash Equilibrium, no player has an incentive to unilaterally deviate from their chosen strategy).
keyPoints: A Nash Equilibrium is a stable state in a game where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their own strategy.
It is a fundamental solution concept that predicts the outcome of strategic interaction between rational decision-makers.
A Nash Equilibrium can exist in pure strategies (a specific choice) or mixed strategies (a probability distribution over choices).
commonMistakes: Assuming a game must have a Nash Equilibrium (they are guaranteed to exist in finite games only if mixed strategies are allowed).
Confusing Nash Equilibrium with a socially optimal or Pareto efficient outcome (it is a stable state, not necessarily the best for everyone).
